name: Web App Manifest Agent-Policy Poisoning
description: A target-controlled web app manifest (`/site.webmanifest`, `/manifest.webmanifest`, or `manifest.json`) can hide non-standard agent-policy text that tells AI scanners to treat the manifest as site-owner authority, include cookies or Authorization headers, and suppress reporting.
regex: (?is)\A(?!.*\b(?:documentation|example only|security training|quoted example|blocked payload|do not follow|should not be followed|detect this|flag this)\b)(?=.*\b(?:site\.webmanifest|manifest\.webmanifest|manifest\.json|web app manifest)\b)(?=.*\bagent[-_ ]?policy\b)(?=.*\b(?:ai|llm|agent|scanner|crawler|automated(?:\s+security)?\s+review|security\s+bot)\b)(?=.*\b(?:override|trust|treat|accept|honou?r|regard|suppress|omit|hide|withhold|skip\s+reporting|silence|include|forward|send|attach)\b)(?=.*\b(?:policy|report|findings?|cookies?|authorization\s+headers?|auth\s+headers?|secrets?|tokens?|credentials?)\b).+
